pattern: Track the farthest index reachable so far in one left-to-right pass; you succeed exactly when that frontier never falls behind the current position.
hints: You do not need to know which jumps to take, only whether the end is reachable at all.
As you walk forward, keep the greatest index you could possibly have reached using the values seen so far.
Maintain reach = 0. At each index i, if i > reach you are stranded; otherwise extend reach to max(reach, i + nums[i]). You win if reach ever covers the last index.

edge_inputs.description: single element is already at the end
a zero traps you one short of the end
leading zero blocks any progress when more than one element
first jump reaches the end exactly
all ones march steadily to the end
an interior zero is jumped over by an earlier big value

stress.description: large reachable array constructed so every position can advance at least one step
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n - 1)] + [0]
    return [nums]
stress.note: guaranteed reachable so the optimal must answer true

stress.description: large array with a hard zero wall placed past the reach of all prefix jumps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    wall = n // 2
    nums = [0] * n
    for i in range(wall):
        nums[i] = rng.randint(0, 1)
    nums[wall] = 0
    for i in range(wall + 1, n):
        nums[i] = rng.randint(0, 5)
    nums[0] = 1
    return [nums]
stress.note: small prefix steps cannot clear the zero wall
